entries.position_summary: The country calls for restoring full Ukrainian control over the Zaporizhzhya nuclear power plant, enabling regular rotation of Ukrainian and IAEA personnel, and reconnecting automatic radiation monitoring data transmission to the IAEA. It urges all parties to refrain from actions that could trigger a nuclear incident and supports sustained IAEA oversight for safety and security.
entries.quote: the territory under Ukraine’s control, provide for regular rotation of Ukrainian and IAEA personnel, reconnect automatic data transmission of all radiation monitoring systems, refrain from taking any actions that could result in a nuclear incident directly to the IAEA, and return full control of Zaporizhzhya to the competent Ukrainian authorities.

entries.position_summary: The statement condemns Russia’s war against Ukraine and its irresponsible nuclear rhetoric, criticizes reckless actions at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant as endangering nuclear facilities and safety, and rejects Russia’s claimed suspension of New START as contrary to international law.
entries.quote: Russia’s unprovoked war against Ukraine tragically continues, as do Russia’s irresponsible nuclear rhetoric, its reckless actions at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, and its claim to suspend the New START Treaty, a claim that is inconsistent with international law.

entries.position_summary: The country calls on Russia to withdraw weapons, equipment, and personnel from the Zaporizhzhya Nuclear Power Plant and restore full control to Ukrainian authorities. This underscores concerns about the safety and security of nuclear facilities in the context of the war in Ukraine.
entries.quote: We continue to call on Russia to remove its weapons, military equipment, and personnel from Zaporizhzhya and return full control of ZNPP to the competent Ukrainian authorities.

entries.position_summary: The country asserts that Russia has violated Director General Grossi’s seven pillars and five principles at the Zaporizhzhya Nuclear Power Plant, creating serious safety and security risks. It further contends that Russia is obstructing full, timely, and unfettered IAEA access to ZNPP, impeding assessment of the plant and implementation of Ukraine’s safeguards agreement.
entries.quote: Director General Grossi’s seven indispensable pillars of nuclear safety and security. They have also contravened his five principles for averting “a catastrophic incident” at the Zaporizhzhya plant. Russia’s actions also disrupt the IAEA’s efforts to secure full, timely, and unfettered access to all areas of ZNPP so that they can assess the safety and security of the plant and implement Ukraine’s safeguards agreement.

entries.position_summary: The delegation condemns Syria’s safeguards non-compliance, the DPRK’s unchecked nuclear and ballistic advances, and Russia’s seizure of Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia NPP, urging accountability for actions undermining the nonproliferation regime. It criticizes Russia and the PRC for expanding and diversifying their nuclear arsenals while showing little interest in arms control, and warns about destabilizing coordination among Russia, the PRC, the DPRK, and Iran.
entries.quote: agreement, Syria’s non-compliance with its comprehensive safeguards agreement, the DPRK’s unchecked advances of its nuclear and ballistic weapons programs, and Russia’s seizure of Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant. Furthermore, Russia and the PRC are expanding and diversifying their nuclear weapons arsenals — showing little or no interest in arms control. Russia, the PRC, and the DPRK, together with Iran, are increasingly cooperating and coordinating with each other in ways that run counter to peace and stability and exacerbate regional tensions. We will have more to say on these topics in our statement on regional issues; however, my delegation wishes to stress that part of keeping the nonproliferation regime strong is holding those whose actions contradict it accountable.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns Russia’s actions in Ukraine, accusing it of seizing peaceful nuclear facilities and engaging in reckless nuclear rhetoric. It also asserts that Russia has violated provisions of the New START Treaty.
entries.quote: • Russia, an NPT nuclear-weapon state and treaty depository, is engaged in an illegal war against its non-nuclear weapon state neighbor. It has violently seized Ukraine’s peaceful nuclear facilities; engaged in reckless nuclear rhetoric; violated provisions of the New START Treaty; and may be

entries.position_summary: They call on Russia to withdraw its weapons, equipment, and personnel from the Zaporizhzhya nuclear plant and to ensure an uninterrupted power supply to all of Ukraine’s nuclear facilities. The statement emphasizes protecting nuclear installations during the conflict and upholding nuclear safety and security.
entries.quote: We call on Russia to remove its weapons and military equipment and civil and military personnel from Zaporizhzhya, maintain an uninterrupted power supply to all of Ukraine’s nuclear facilities from

entries.position_summary: The United States denies responsibility for destabilizing actions and implicitly condemns Russia for invading a neighbor, undermining decades of bilateral arms control progress, engaging in dangerous nuclear rhetoric, endangering civilian nuclear facilities, and moving nuclear weapons closer to others’ borders in the context of the war in Ukraine.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, It is not the United States that has attacked a neighboring country without provocation. It is not the United States that has undermined a half century of progress on bilateral arms control, engaged in irresponsible and dangerous nuclear rhetoric, endangered civilian nuclear energy facilities, or moved nuclear weapons closer to others’ borders.

entries.position_summary: The country underscores that robust nuclear security, alongside IAEA safeguards, is essential to prevent theft, trafficking, and misuse of nuclear materials and technologies. It calls for the highest standards of nuclear safety and security as peaceful nuclear applications and emerging technologies grow. It supports the IAEA’s work to uphold safety and security in Ukraine and condemns actions that jeopardize these efforts.

entries.position_summary: The statement argues that Russia’s war against Ukraine endangers Ukrainian nuclear facilities, threatening safety, security, and safeguards, which could erode public trust in nuclear technology and undermine peaceful applications. It warns that such damage would harm global development and climate efforts and negatively impact the NPT, with responsibility attributed to Russia.
entries.quote: Global efforts to promote and support the growth of nuclear power with the highest standards of safety, security, and safeguards is, however, put at risk because of Russia’s war against Ukraine. Any damage to Ukrainian nuclear reactors would feed into public distrust of nuclear technology in general and challenge public acceptance of even the non-power applications of nuclear science and technology in areas like food safety, health, and water resource management. Damage to Ukrainian facilities would damage global development, damage efforts to address climate change, and damage the NPT – and that would be on Russia.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns Russia’s attacks and seizure of Ukraine’s nuclear facilities, asserting that these actions undermine Ukraine’s peaceful nuclear energy program and compromise nuclear safety and security. It frames the situation as a direct threat to the peaceful uses of nuclear technology in Ukraine.
